Class Position

java.lang.Object
core.Position
public class Position extends Object
Represents a specific coordinate on the 8x8 game board. This class is immutable; once a position is created, its coordinates cannot change.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Position(int row, int col)
    Constructs a new Position with the specified row and column.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    boolean
    equals(Object o)
    Compares this position to the specified object.
    int
    getCol()
    Retrieves the column coordinate of this position.
    int
    getRow()
    Retrieves the row coordinate of this position.
    int
    hashCode()
    Generates a hash code for this position, allowing it to be used effectively in hash-based collections like HashSet or HashMap.
    boolean
    isValid()
    Determines whether the position falls within the boundaries of an 8x8 board.
    Position
    offset(int dr, int dc)
    Creates a new Position offset by the given directional values.
    String
    toString()
    Converts the internal 0-indexed integer coordinates into standard algebraic game notation (e.g., "a8", "e4", "h1").

    Methods inherited from class Object

    clone, finalize, getClass, notify, notifyAll, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • Position

      public Position(int row, int col)
      Constructs a new Position with the specified row and column.
      Parameters:
      row - the zero-indexed row coordinate
      col - the zero-indexed column coordinate

  • Method Details

    • getRow

      public int getRow()
      Retrieves the row coordinate of this position.
      Returns:
      the row index

    • getCol

      public int getCol()
      Retrieves the column coordinate of this position.
      Returns:
      the column index

    • isValid

      public boolean isValid()
      Determines whether the position falls within the boundaries of an 8x8 board.
      Returns:
      true if both the row and column are between 0 and 7 (inclusive), false otherwise

    • offset

      public Position offset(int dr, int dc)
      Creates a new Position offset by the given directional values. This is highly useful for calculating piece movement trajectories.
      Parameters:
      dr - the change in the row direction
      dc - the change in the column direction
      Returns:
      a new Position instance with the offset applied

    • equals

      public boolean equals(Object o)
      Compares this position to the specified object. Two positions are considered equal if they share the exact same row and column.
      Overrides:
      equals in class Object
      Parameters:
      o - the object to compare against
      Returns:
      true if the given object represents the same grid coordinate, false otherwise

    • hashCode

      public int hashCode()
      Generates a hash code for this position, allowing it to be used effectively in hash-based collections like HashSet or HashMap.
      Overrides:
      hashCode in class Object
      Returns:
      the hash code value based on the row and column

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Converts the internal 0-indexed integer coordinates into standard algebraic game notation (e.g., "a8", "e4", "h1").
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      the string representation of the position in algebraic notation
